title: Building real-time user insights into your design workflow
key_points: Launched Marvin Live Intercept, billed as the world's first live in-app AI interview intercept: JavaScript embedded in any website/product waits for a behavioral trigger (e.g., user repeatedly clicking the same button, navigating back and forth, or landing on a new app), then prompts a real-time screen-share interview.

The AI interviewer records the user's screen, asks intelligent follow-up questions in 40 different languages, then sends videos back to the Marvin research-analysis account where they can be shared in real time via Slack, Teams, Figma, etc.

Core thesis: scaling/speeding up research (faster surveys, AI moderation) is just 'a faster horse.' The real unsolved problem is capturing user insight in the exact moment before a decision is made, closing the gap between insight and decision-making.

Critiques existing methods: in-app surveys have abysmally low response rates and low fidelity because they force users to translate their mental model into words; after-the-fact interviews force users to reconstruct experiences from memory weeks later. In-the-moment, screen-shared feedback gives completely different fidelity.

Vision framed as 'one researcher per user', letting designers talk to users at the moment of need to match engineering pace without leaving users behind. Origin story: his wife sat on a Zoom call for days waiting for users to click a feedback pop-up, exposing the friction problem.

quotes.text: everyone is pitching it including ourselves how it's a way to get interviewing faster but in the end we're building a faster horse
quotes.talk: Building real-time user insights into your design workflow

quotes.text: The best feedback comes from the moment someone is in your product, not in a survey that they fill out later
quotes.talk: Building real-time user insights into your design workflow
